councils.acquisition_path_summary: HTML form submission with postcode/street/house name. Web scraping required. Community reports Whitespace backend but no public API exposed. UKBinCollectionData project has working scraper.
councils.notes: Uses Whitespace backend for waste management (per community sources). Form accepts postcode, street name, or house name. No visible JSON/API endpoints in public interface. Would require form POST automation and HTML parsing of results.
councils.robots_caution: Check robots.txt. Site may reject automated access.
councils.terms_caution: Review terms of service for automated access restrictions.
councils.security_notes: Standard council site with no apparent rate limiting visible. Input sanitisation required on all form fields.
councils.recommended_adapter_approach: HTML scraping with form automation. Consider using existing UKBinCollectionData patterns as reference. Fallback to browser automation if scraping breaks.
councils.brittleness_concerns: HTML structure changes would break adapter. No API contract. Community scraper exists as fallback pattern.
councils.rate_limit_guidance: Unknown - respect reasonable throttling (1-2 req/sec max). Monitor for 429 or blocks.

councils.acquisition_path_summary: Address lookup via maps.easthants.gov.uk 'where I live' service returns calendar number. Then downloadable PDF calendars (13-month periods) per collection round. Structured but not machine-readable.
councils.notes: Digital-first approach with PDF calendars instead of paper. Calendar numbers assigned per collection round. Requires: (1) address → calendar number lookup, (2) PDF calendar download/parse. PDFs are structured by weekday and area.
councils.security_notes: Map-based lookup may have CAPTCHA or bot detection. PDF parsing adds complexity.
councils.recommended_adapter_approach: Two-phase: (1) Automate address→calendar lookup via 'where I live', (2) PDF download and OCR/structured parse. PDFs are predictable format. Consider caching calendars (13-month validity).
councils.brittleness_concerns: PDF format changes would require parser updates. Map lookup service changes. Calendar numbering system changes.
councils.rate_limit_guidance: Respectful polling. Cache calendar PDFs locally once downloaded (13-month lifetime).

councils.acquisition_path_summary: UPRN-based endpoint at my.eastleigh.gov.uk/apex/EBC_Waste_Calendar?UPRN=<uprn>. Two-step: (1) postcode → UPRN lookup, (2) UPRN query returns collection calendar. Oracle APEX backend.
councils.notes: EXCELLENT CANDIDATE. Known working endpoint: https://my.eastleigh.gov.uk/apex/EBC_Waste_Calendar?UPRN=100060321174. Oracle APEX application. Community reports recent bot protection added (403 errors from some scrapers). MyEastleigh account system exists but UPRN endpoint may work unauthenticated. Requires UPRN resolution first (can use OS AddressBase or council postcode lookup).
councils.robots_caution: Recent reports of 403 blocks on automated access. Check robots.txt. May need User-Agent rotation.
councils.terms_caution: Verify terms - endpoint appears public but may have usage restrictions.
councils.security_notes: Bot protection recently added per community reports. May require session cookies or User-Agent mimicking browser. UPRN validation required. Oracle APEX applications sometimes have CSRF tokens.
councils.recommended_adapter_approach: UPRN lookup → query endpoint. Implement proper User-Agent and session management to avoid 403. Consider using headless browser if direct HTTP fails. Cache responses aggressively (collections change infrequently). Test authentication requirements.
councils.brittleness_concerns: Bot protection may tighten. Oracle APEX application could change endpoints. UPRN requirement adds lookup dependency.
councils.rate_limit_guidance: Conservative - max 1 req/2sec. Implement exponential backoff on 403. Cache heavily (collection schedules stable for weeks).

councils.acquisition_path_summary: Bartec Collective platform. SOAP-based API (Bartec Collective API) with Features_Get endpoint. Public dashboard at farehamgw.bartecmunicipal.com. Authentication required for API access.
councils.notes: BARTEC PLATFORM IDENTIFIED. Uses Bartec Municipal Technologies Collective system. Public dashboard exists (farehamgw.bartecmunicipal.com). Official SOAP API available but requires authentication token. API endpoint example: collectiveapi.bartec-systems.com/API-R1531/CollectiveAPI.asmx. Methods include Features_Get (collection dates by UPRN). May need council partnership for API credentials. Web scraping of public dashboard possible as alternative.
councils.robots_caution: Check robots.txt for both council domain and bartecmunicipal.com.
councils.terms_caution: API requires credentials - likely need formal council agreement. Public dashboard may be scrapable.
councils.security_notes: SOAP API uses token authentication. Dashboard may have different auth. Bartec platforms vary in security config per council. Test public dashboard access level first.
councils.recommended_adapter_approach: Option A: Request API credentials from council (proper but bureaucratic). Option B: Scrape public Bartec dashboard (farehamgw.bartecmunicipal.com) if accessible. Option C: Screen-scrape council website form. Bartec adapter pattern reusable across councils.
councils.brittleness_concerns: API credentials may expire or require renewal. Dashboard UI changes. Bartec version upgrades could change response format.
councils.rate_limit_guidance: If using API: follow Bartec docs. If scraping dashboard: max 1 req/2sec, respect 429 responses.

councils.acquisition_path_summary: Postcode search form. Cookie consent banner present. Downloadable PDF calendars for 2025/26. No visible API. Likely requires form automation and HTML parsing.
councils.notes: Simple postcode lookup form (requires space in postcode). Cookie consent banner detected. Provides downloadable annual PDF calendar. No API identified. Standard web form automation pattern. PDF calendar is year-long (can be cached).
councils.robots_caution: Check robots.txt. Cookie consent banner may complicate automation.
councils.security_notes: Cookie consent may require interaction. Form likely has CSRF protection. Input sanitisation required.
councils.recommended_adapter_approach: Form POST automation with postcode. Handle cookie consent. Parse HTML response or download/parse PDF calendar for year-ahead data. PDF parsing adds reliability.
councils.brittleness_concerns: HTML structure changes. Cookie consent banner changes. PDF format changes (less likely).
councils.rate_limit_guidance: Conservative throttling. 1 req/2sec max. Monitor for blocks.

councils.acquisition_path_summary: Postcode form lookup. Year-round calendar download available. Alternative 'In your area' map tool (maps.hart.gov.uk). No visible API. Backend platform unconfirmed.
councils.notes: Postcode lookup returns collection day and downloadable year-round calendar. Also offers map-based lookup via maps.hart.gov.uk/mycouncil.aspx. No public info on backend platform (not Whitespace, Bartec, or Alloy confirmed). Standard form automation pattern.
councils.robots_caution: Check robots.txt for main site and maps subdomain.
councils.security_notes: Form may have CSRF. Map tool may have bot detection.
councils.recommended_adapter_approach: Form automation with postcode → parse HTML response or download calendar. Map tool as alternative data source if main form blocks automation. Calendar download preferred for caching.
councils.brittleness_concerns: HTML changes. Form parameters change. Map tool dependency if primary fails.
councils.rate_limit_guidance: Throttle to 1-2 req/sec. Monitor for blocks.

councils.acquisition_path_summary: Postcode/address lookup. North/South area PDF calendars available. Alternate weekly kerbside service. No visible API. Standard form automation required.
councils.notes: Postcode or address entry required. Downloadable calendars split by North/South areas for 2026. Alternate weekly collections (rubbish one week, recycling next). Food waste starting Spring 2026. No API identified. Standard council web form.
councils.security_notes: Form likely has standard CSRF protections. Input validation required.
councils.recommended_adapter_approach: Form automation with postcode/address → HTML parsing. PDF calendars as supplementary data source (year-ahead cache). Need to handle North/South area distinction.
councils.brittleness_concerns: HTML structure changes. North/South area system changes. Calendar format changes.

councils.acquisition_path_summary: Postcode lookup service. Phased rollout of new wheelie bin service (2025-26) with phase checker tool. Site returned 403 on fetch attempt - possible bot protection. Rural district with complex phasing.
councils.notes: CAUTION: 403 forbidden on automated fetch attempt. Site may have aggressive bot protection. Phased service changes 2025-26 with different collection patterns per phase. Postcode checker to determine phase. Rural district with varied geography. May require browser automation due to bot protection.
councils.robots_caution: 403 encountered - likely bot protection active. Check robots.txt carefully.
councils.terms_caution: Site appears to actively block automated access. Manual testing required.
councils.security_notes: Active bot protection confirmed (403 responses). May require headless browser, session management, CAPTCHA handling. Phasing system adds complexity.
councils.recommended_adapter_approach: Browser automation likely required due to 403 blocks. Playwright/Puppeteer for postcode lookup. Handle phase detection. Complex due to phased rollout. Consider postponing until service stabilises post-rollout.
councils.brittleness_concerns: Bot protection. Phasing system complexity. Service changes during rollout. 403 blocks make reliability uncertain.
councils.rate_limit_guidance: Very conservative if access achievable. Consider read-only caching. High risk of blocks.

councils.acquisition_path_summary: Granicus-powered 'My Portsmouth' customer account portal. Form-based lookup (postcode + house number). No public API identified. Community scrapers exist in UKBinCollectionData.
councils.notes: Uses Granicus platform for customer portal (my.portsmouth.gov.uk). Form accepts postcode and house number. No documented API. XHR inspection possible but not published. Community has working scrapers (UKBinCollectionData). Large urban authority - likely stable collection patterns. Portal requires cookie consent.
councils.terms_caution: Granicus platform may have specific ToS. Review automated access policy.
councils.security_notes: Granicus portal - likely has session management, CSRF tokens. Cookie policy consent required. Portal authentication may be optional for lookup.
councils.recommended_adapter_approach: Form automation via My Portsmouth portal. Handle Granicus session/cookies. Inspect XHR traffic to find underlying API calls. Community scrapers exist as reference. Consider headless browser if form automation fails.
councils.brittleness_concerns: Granicus platform updates. Form structure changes. Session requirements tightening.
councils.rate_limit_guidance: Respectful throttling. 1-2 req/sec. Monitor for blocks from Granicus infrastructure.

councils.acquisition_path_summary: Bin collection day finder with postcode/street name input. Calendar download available. Clean form interface. No visible API.
councils.notes: Clean postcode/street name lookup form. Downloadable calendar available. Shows rubbish (green bin), recycling (blue bin), glass, food waste (weekly), and garden waste. Straightforward form automation pattern. No API visible.
councils.security_notes: Standard form protections expected. Input sanitisation required.
councils.recommended_adapter_approach: Direct form automation - postcode/street name → parse HTML response. Calendar download as cache mechanism. Relatively straightforward pattern. Good test case for form automation approach.
councils.brittleness_concerns: HTML structure changes. Form parameters change. Calendar format changes.
councils.rate_limit_guidance: Standard throttling 1-2 req/sec. Low-risk profile suggests reasonable tolerance.

councils.acquisition_path_summary: Bin collection calendar with postcode/street name input. Site uses Incapsula (Imperva) bot protection. 403 blocks likely. Third-party UPRN service exists (bin-calendar.nova.do).
councils.notes: CAUTION: Incapsula/Imperva bot protection detected on site. Likely to block automated access. Calendar requires postcode/street name (min 6 chars). Third-party service 'Bin Calendar' (bin-calendar.nova.do) offers UPRN-based lookup for Southampton - may be more reliable than direct council access. Large urban authority. Consider third-party proxy or browser automation.
councils.robots_caution: Incapsula protection active. Check robots.txt but expect aggressive blocking.
councils.terms_caution: Bot protection suggests automated access discouraged. Review policy carefully.
councils.security_notes: Incapsula/Imperva CDN protection. Likely CAPTCHA, rate limiting, bot detection. High security posture. Third-party bin-calendar service may be workaround.
councils.recommended_adapter_approach: Direct access difficult due to Incapsula. Options: (1) Browser automation with Playwright, (2) Use third-party bin-calendar.nova.do UPRN service, (3) UPRN lookup alternative. Third-party service likely more stable short-term. Consider this lower priority until security posture understood.
councils.brittleness_concerns: Incapsula protection may block all automation. Third-party service dependency if using bin-calendar. CAPTCHA challenges likely. High maintenance risk.
councils.rate_limit_guidance: If attempting direct access: extremely conservative, expect blocks. Third-party service: follow their rate limits.

councils.acquisition_path_summary: Postcode lookup form. Alternate weekly collections (black bin one week, brown recycling next). My Test Valley portal exists but no API documented. Community tools available.
councils.notes: Standard postcode lookup. Alternate weekly service (black household waste, brown recycling). My Test Valley app/portal exists (my.testvalley.gov.uk) but no public API. Form automation pattern. Bins must be out by 7am. No downloadable calendar identified.
councils.robots_caution: Check robots.txt for main site and my.testvalley subdomain.
councils.security_notes: Standard form protections. My Test Valley portal may have separate auth requirements.
councils.recommended_adapter_approach: Form automation with postcode → parse HTML response. Check My Test Valley portal for potential API endpoints. Community tools (UKBinCollectionData) may have working implementations as reference.
councils.brittleness_concerns: HTML structure changes. No calendar download complicates caching. Portal changes could affect lookup.
councils.rate_limit_guidance: Standard throttling 1-2 req/sec. Monitor for blocks.

councils.acquisition_path_summary: React SPA (/icollectionday/). JavaScript-rendered, requires browser execution. Community PWA exists as reference (bin-collection-app on GitHub). My Winchester portal (my.winchester.gov.uk/icollectionday/).
councils.notes: JAVASCRIPT SPA - React application at /icollectionday/. HTML fetch returns empty shell - full JS execution required. No API visible in initial load. Community has built React/TypeScript PWA (github.com/EnterTheVortex2/bin-collection-app) which demonstrates collection day logic. Backend likely has API but not documented. Browser automation required OR XHR endpoint discovery via network inspection. my.winchester.gov.uk/icollectionday/ may expose endpoints.
councils.robots_caution: Check robots.txt for main site and my.winchester subdomain. JavaScript rendering may bypass some checks.
councils.terms_caution: React SPA suggests modern architecture - check API usage policy if endpoints discovered.
councils.security_notes: React SPA likely calls backend API - need network inspection to find endpoints. May have auth tokens, CSRF protection. Community PWA exists as proof of concept. Browser automation may be more reliable than API reverse-engineering.
councils.recommended_adapter_approach: Browser automation with Playwright/Puppeteer for postcode lookup → extract results from rendered page. Alternative: XHR inspection to find API endpoints (likely exist but undocumented). Community PWA code (github) as reference for data structures. Calendar download available which could be cached.
councils.brittleness_concerns: React application updates. API endpoints change (if discovered). Calendar format changes. Browser automation overhead.
councils.rate_limit_guidance: If using browser automation: slow and respectful (5-10 sec between requests). If API discovered: standard throttling 1-2 req/sec.
